openmeter/billing/creditgrant.purpose: Credit-grant-centric facade over the charges layer that exposes Create, Get, List, and UpdateExternalSettlement operations for creditpurchase.Charge values; owns input validation and customer-ownership checks but holds no Ent client of its own.

patterns.name: Config+Validate/New constructor
patterns.description: Every concrete implementation uses a Config struct with a Validate() method; New() calls Validate() before returning the service.
patterns.example: type Config struct{ ChargesService charges.Service }; func (c Config) Validate() error { ... }; func New(c Config) (Service, error) { if err := c.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } }

patterns.name: Input.Validate() called at top of every method
patterns.description: Each public service method calls input.Validate() before any business logic and wraps errors in models.NewNillableGenericValidationError.
patterns.example: func (s *service) Get(ctx context.Context, input GetInput) (creditpurchase.Charge, error) { if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return ..., err } ... }

patterns.name: Delegate all persistence to charges.Service
patterns.description: No Ent imports appear in this package; all DB access flows through charges.Service or creditpurchase.Service injected via Config.
patterns.example: charge, err := s.chargesService.GetByID(ctx, ...)

patterns.name: Customer ownership check in service layer
patterns.description: Get-style methods assert cpCharge.Intent.CustomerID == input.CustomerID after fetching; not enforced by charges.Service.
patterns.example: if charge.Intent.CustomerID != input.CustomerID { return ..., models.NewGenericForbiddenError(...) }

patterns.name: NoopService compile-time guard
patterns.description: noop.go provides NoopService{} with var _ Service = NoopService{} ensuring interface compliance without business logic.
patterns.example: var _ Service = NoopService{}

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Service interface, all Input types with Validate(), FundingMethod enum, PurchaseTerms, GrantFilters. Also holds the concrete service struct and New constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Input.Validate() must call models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)) — never return raw errors from validation.

key_file_guides.role: No-op implementation used when credit grants are disabled; returns zero-values with nil errors for all methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must stay in sync with the Service interface; update when new methods are added.

anti_patterns: Importing openmeter/ent/db or calling Ent builders directly — all DB access must flow through charges.Service
Skipping input.Validate() at the top of a new method
Returning a charge without verifying CustomerID ownership in Get-style methods
Using context.Background() instead of the caller-supplied ctx
Adding business logic inside toIntent()/toSettlement() conversion helpers beyond pure field mapping

decisions.decision: Service delegates entirely to charges.Service rather than holding its own adapter
decisions.rationale: Keeps a single authoritative write path for credit-purchase charges; avoids parallel DB access patterns that could bypass transaction discipline.

decisions.decision: Ownership check done in the service layer, not in charges.Service
decisions.rationale: charges.Service is a generic multi-customer service; customer scoping is a caller responsibility enforced here.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new service method with input validation and delegation

openmeter/billing/httpdriver.purpose: v1 HTTP handler package for the billing domain — translates between api.* types and billing.* service inputs/outputs, registers the billing errorEncoder, and exposes the composite Handler interface consumed by openmeter/server/router.

patterns.name: Handler method returns typed httptransport.HandlerWithArgs or httptransport.Handler
patterns.description: Each HTTP operation is a method on *handler that returns an httptransport.Handler[Req,Resp] or httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[Req,Resp,Params]. The handler method only declares the type, never calls ServeHTTP.
patterns.example: func (h *handler) GetProfile() GetProfileHandler { return httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decode, operate, encode, opts...) }

patterns.name: Type alias triple for every endpoint
patterns.description: Each endpoint declares Request, Response, and Params type aliases at the top of its function block or as package-level types, then a Handler type alias for the httptransport.Handler generic.
patterns.example: type ( GetProfileRequest = billing.GetProfileInput; GetProfileResponse = api.BillingProfile; GetProfileParams struct { ID string; Expand []api.BillingProfileExpand }; GetProfileHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[...] )

patterns.name: resolveNamespace helper for all decoders
patterns.description: Every decode function calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) first; an InternalServerError is returned if the namespace is missing.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ..., fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve namespace: %w", err) }

patterns.name: errorEncoder() composed from billing domain errors
patterns.description: errors.go defines errorEncoder() returning a chain: billing.NotFoundError→404, billing.ValidationError→400, billing.UpdateAfterDeleteError→409, billing.ValidationIssue→400, billing.AppError→400.
patterns.example: httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(errorEncoder()))

patterns.name: mapAndValidateInvoiceLineRateCardDeprecatedFields for dual-field parsing
patterns.description: When an endpoint accepts both rateCard and deprecated top-level price/featureKey/taxConfig fields, use mapAndValidateInvoiceLineRateCardDeprecatedFields to resolve them consistently.
patterns.example: rateCardParsed, err := mapAndValidateInvoiceLineRateCardDeprecatedFields(invoiceLineRateCardItems{ RateCard: line.RateCard, Price: line.Price, ... })

key_file_guides.role: Defines the composite Handler interface (ProfileHandler + InvoiceLineHandler + InvoiceHandler + CustomerOverrideHandler), the handler struct, and the New() constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New methods on the Handler interface must also be implemented on *handler and registered in router.Config.

key_file_guides.role: Single errorEncoder() for all billing HTTP endpoints; maps billing-domain error types to HTTP status codes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New billing domain error types must be added here; omitting them causes 500 responses.

key_file_guides.role: Handles backward-compatibility parsing of legacy price/featureKey/taxConfig fields alongside the newer rateCard field.
key_file_guides.watch_for: When new invoice line fields are deprecated, add their bridge logic here; never inline dual-field logic into endpoint decoders.

key_file_guides.role: All invoice-level HTTP operations: List, Get, Delete, Update, InvoicePendingLines, Simulate, ProgressInvoice (approve/retry/advance/snapshot_quantities).
key_file_guides.watch_for: ProgressInvoice uses a ProgressAction discriminator — add new actions to both InvoiceProgressActions and invoiceProgressOperationNames.

key_file_guides.role: Profile CRUD and MapProfileToApi; also contains fromAPIBillingWorkflow, resolveProfileApps, app reference mapping.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MapProfileToApi has two code paths (p.Apps set vs p.AppReferences) — keep both in sync when adding profile fields.

key_file_guides.role: Package-level pagination and display defaults (DefaultPageSize=100, DefaultPageNumber=1, etc.).
key_file_guides.watch_for: All list handlers must use these defaults via lo.FromPtrOr — never hard-code page size inline.

anti_patterns: Calling billing.Service methods directly from a decode function — decode must only parse, operate must call the service
Returning billing.ValidationError without wrapping in errorEncoder — will cause a 500
Adding new Handler interface methods without adding them to handler.go's interface definition
Inlining deprecated-field compatibility logic outside deprecations.go
Using context.Background() instead of the caller's ctx in any handler closure

decisions.decision: Two-closure httptransport.Handler pattern (decode + operate) rather than a single ServeHTTP method
decisions.rationale: Separates namespace/input parsing from business logic; allows the httptransport framework to handle error encoding and OTel tracing uniformly.

decisions.decision: errorEncoder() defined once in errors.go, appended to every handler's options
decisions.rationale: Ensures consistent HTTP status codes for billing domain errors across all endpoints without repeating type-switch logic.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new billing HTTP endpoint

openmeter/billing/lineengine.purpose: Implements the billing.LineEngine interface for the standard invoice line type — handles quantity snapshotting, split-line-group creation, line period truncation, and per-line rating via rating.Service; registered as LineEngineTypeInvoice in the billing.Service engine registry.

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate() + New()
patterns.description: Engine is constructed via Config.Validate() + New(Config), returning (*Engine, error). All dependencies injected through Config.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (*Engine, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } return &Engine{...}, nil }

patterns.name: SplitLineGroupAdapter interface for DB isolation
patterns.description: splitlinegroup.go defines SplitLineGroupAdapter with only CreateSplitLineGroup and GetSplitLineGroupHeaders; Engine never imports the full billing.Adapter.
patterns.example: type SplitLineGroupAdapter interface { CreateSplitLineGroup(...) (billing.SplitLineGroup, error); GetSplitLineGroupHeaders(...) (billing.SplitLineGroupHeaders, error) }

patterns.name: QuantitySnapshotter interface for dependency inversion
patterns.description: stdinvoice.go defines QuantitySnapshotter with SnapshotLineQuantities; concrete implementation is billingservice.Service. Engine never calls the service directly.
patterns.example: type QuantitySnapshotter interface { SnapshotLineQuantities(ctx context.Context, invoice billing.StandardInvoice, lines billing.StandardLines) error }

patterns.name: billing.ValidationIssue for snapshot errors, not plain errors
patterns.description: When SnapshotLineQuantities returns an ErrSnapshotInvalidDatabaseState, OnCollectionCompleted converts it to billing.ValidationIssue with ValidationIssueSeverityCritical.
patterns.example: if _, isInvalidDatabaseState := lo.ErrorsAs[*billing.ErrSnapshotInvalidDatabaseState](err); isInvalidDatabaseState { return nil, billing.ValidationIssue{Severity: billing.ValidationIssueSeverityCritical, ...} }

patterns.name: isPeriodEmptyConsideringTruncations for period validity
patterns.description: Usage-based lines check if their period is empty after truncation to streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration; flat-fee lines always return false.
patterns.example: return line.GetServicePeriod().Truncate(streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration).IsEmpty(), nil

key_file_guides.role: Implements billing.LineEngine interface: GetLineEngineType, OnCollectionCompleted, OnStandardInvoiceCreated, OnInvoiceIssued, OnPaymentAuthorized, OnPaymentSettled. OnCollectionCompleted is the primary hook for quantity snapshotting.
key_file_guides.watch_for: OnCollectionCompleted short-circuits if QuantitySnapshotedAt is already set and at or after the default collection time — do not add snapshot logic after this guard.

key_file_guides.role: SplitGatheringLine splits a gathering line at a point in time, creates a SplitLineGroup if needed, and produces pre/post split line pairs. ResolveSplitLineGroupHeaders batch-fetches group metadata for standard lines.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ChildUniqueReferenceID is set to nil on split lines — the SplitLineGroup owns this reference; never copy it from the source line.

key_file_guides.role: BuildStandardInvoiceLines converts gathering lines to standard lines, snapshots quantities, and runs CalculateLines. CalculateLines calls ratingService.GenerateDetailedLines per line.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CalculateLines requires lines to be non-empty — guard callers; invoicecalc.MergeGeneratedDetailedLines is called after each GenerateDetailedLines call.

anti_patterns: Importing billing.Adapter or openmeter/ent/db directly — use SplitLineGroupAdapter interface
Calling SnapshotLineQuantities from outside OnCollectionCompleted/BuildStandardInvoiceLines — quantity snapshotting is lifecycle-gated
Skipping ResolveSplitLineGroupHeaders before CalculateLines on lines that may have split groups — price calculations depend on SplitLineHierarchy being populated
Returning plain errors from snapshot paths that should be billing.ValidationIssue — callers use type assertions on ValidationIssue
Copying ChildUniqueReferenceID from source gathering line to split child lines

decisions.decision: Engine registered at Service construction time via Service.RegisterLineEngine, not hardcoded
decisions.rationale: Allows charges sub-packages to register their own engines (flatfee, usagebased, creditpurchase) without modifying the base service.

decisions.decision: SplitLineGroupAdapter and QuantitySnapshotter are separate narrow interfaces rather than the full billing.Service
decisions.rationale: Prevents circular imports; lineengine is imported by billingservice which is the QuantitySnapshotter implementation.

openmeter/billing/models.purpose: Structural container for shared billing domain model sub-packages: creditsapplied (credit slice type with currency-aware summation), externalid (invoice/line external-ID types and Ent mixin helpers), stddetailedline (detailed line base struct with Ent mixin and goderive equality), and totals (financial breakdown struct with Ent mixin and currency-precision rounding). No source files live here directly.

patterns.name: Ent mixin co-located with domain model
patterns.description: Each sub-package defines both the Go domain struct (model.go) and the corresponding Ent mixin (mixin.go) in the same package to keep schema and type in sync.
patterns.example:
// model.go: type Totals struct { ... }
// mixin.go: func (Mixin) Fields() []ent.Field { ... }

patterns.name: Typed Setter/Getter generic interfaces over raw Ent mutations
patterns.description: externalid and totals expose generic Creator[T]/Setter[T]/Getter interfaces so adapters never call individual Set* Ent methods directly.
patterns.example: type Setter[T any] interface { SetTotals(T) *T }; func Set[T any](s Setter[T], t Totals) { ... }

patterns.name: Currency-precision rounding before persistence
patterns.description: Totals.RoundToPrecision(currency) must be called before any DB write to prevent numeric precision drift.
patterns.example: t = t.RoundToPrecision(currencyx.Calculator{Currency: currency})

patterns.name: models.Validator on enums and structs
patterns.description: All enum types and structs in this package implement Validate() error conforming to models.Validator.
patterns.example: func (s Status) Validate() error { switch s { case ...: return nil; default: return fmt.Errorf("invalid status: %s", s) } }

patterns.name: goderive for equality on stddetailedline.Base
patterns.description: derived.gen.go is generated by goderive from annotations in generate.go; never hand-edit the generated file.
patterns.example: //go:generate go run github.com/awalterschulze/goderive . in generate.go

key_file_guides.role: Totals struct with Add, Sum, RoundToPrecision, Validate. Validate enforces non-negative invariants.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a field to Totals requires updating Add, RoundToPrecision, Validate, mixin Fields(), Setter[T], TotalsGetter, Set[T], and FromDB.

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin adding invoicing/payment external ID columns; generic Creator/Updater/Getter interfaces for type-safe Ent mutations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always use lo.EmptyableToPtr for nullable string columns — never store empty strings.

key_file_guides.role: Base struct for flat-fee and usage-based detailed lines. child_unique_reference_id is required (DB CHECK constraint).
key_file_guides.watch_for: child_unique_reference_id has a DB CHECK constraint — it is required for upsert identity; never make it optional.

key_file_guides.role: CreditsApplied slice with Validate (positivity) and currency-aware Sum.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Accumulating amounts without RoundToPrecision causes precision drift.

anti_patterns: Adding DB/Ent logic to model.go or domain logic to mixin.go — keep layers strictly separated
Hand-editing derived.gen.go — always regenerate via go generate
Skipping RoundToPrecision before persisting Totals
Storing empty strings in nullable external-ID columns instead of using lo.EmptyableToPtr
Adding a new Totals field without updating all companion methods (Add, RoundToPrecision, Validate, mixin, Set, FromDB)

decisions.decision: Mixin and domain model co-located in the same sub-package rather than in a shared mixin layer
decisions.rationale: Keeps schema and domain type in sync; a single file change updates both the DB column and its Go representation.

decisions.decision: goderive for equality rather than hand-written Equal methods on stddetailedline.Base
decisions.rationale: Base has deeply nested pointer/slice fields; generated equality is complete and removes maintenance burden.

openmeter/billing/service.purpose: Concrete implementation of billing.Service — the central orchestrator for profiles, customer overrides, gathering invoices, standard invoices, invoice lines, sequences, app resolution, lock acquisition, and the stateless-based invoice state machine. All DB access is delegated to billing.Adapter via entutils transactions.

patterns.name: Config struct validation + New() constructor with RegisterLineEngine
patterns.description: service.go uses Config with Validate(); New() creates the service, instantiates billinglineengine.Engine internally, and calls RegisterLineEngine so the invoice engine is always pre-registered.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (*Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; svc := &Service{...}; invoiceEngine, _ := billinglineengine.New(...); svc.RegisterLineEngine(invoiceEngine); return svc, nil }

patterns.name: transactionForInvoiceManipulation wraps all customer-mutating operations
patterns.description: All methods that create/update invoices or lines call transactionForInvoiceManipulation which first UpsertCustomerLock (outside tx) then starts a transaction and calls LockCustomerForUpdate inside it.
patterns.example: return transactionForInvoiceManipulation(ctx, s, input.Customer, func(ctx context.Context) (T, error) { ... })

patterns.name: transaction.Run / transaction.RunWithNoValue for all adapter calls
patterns.description: Service methods wrap adapter access in transaction.Run or RunWithNoValue so Ent's ctx-bound transaction is propagated correctly.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) (billing.X, error) { return s.adapter.GetX(ctx, ...) })

patterns.name: InvoiceStateMachine + sync.Pool for state machine reuse
patterns.description: stdinvoicestate.go allocates InvoiceStateMachine instances from invoiceStateMachineCache (sync.Pool); external storage binds the state machine to the in-memory Invoice struct.
patterns.example: sm := invoiceStateMachineCache.Get().(*InvoiceStateMachine); defer invoiceStateMachineCache.Put(sm)

patterns.name: advancementStrategy switches between inline and queued advancement
patterns.description: advanceUntilStateStable checks s.advancementStrategy: QueuedAdvancementStrategy publishes an AdvanceStandardInvoiceEvent; otherwise runs sm.AdvanceUntilStateStable inline.
patterns.example: if s.advancementStrategy == billing.QueuedAdvancementStrategy { return s.publisher.Publish(ctx, billing.AdvanceStandardInvoiceEvent{...}) }

patterns.name: featureMetersErrorWrapper converts not-found to ErrSnapshotInvalidDatabaseState
patterns.description: resolveFeatureMeters wraps the returned FeatureMeters in featureMetersErrorWrapper so downstream callers receive ErrSnapshotInvalidDatabaseState instead of generic not-found for billing consistency.
patterns.example: return featureMetersErrorWrapper{featureMeters}, nil

patterns.name: resolveTaxCodes with ReadOnly flag
patterns.description: taxcode.go's resolveTaxCodes uses ReadOnly=true for simulate/preview flows (GetTaxCodeByAppMapping) and ReadOnly=false for real invoice state machine transitions (GetOrCreateByAppMapping).
patterns.example: taxCodes, err := s.resolveTaxCodes(ctx, resolveTaxCodesInput{Namespace: ns, Invoice: out, ReadOnly: true})

key_file_guides.role: Service struct definition, Config validation, New constructor, transactionForInvoiceManipulation helper, WithAdvancementStrategy/WithLockedNamespaces options, RegisterStandardInvoiceHooks.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All invoice-mutating helpers must call transactionForInvoiceManipulation; never start a raw transaction without the customer lock sequence.

key_file_guides.role: InvoiceStateMachine with full state graph: draft → issuing → issued → payment states → paid/overdue/uncollectible/voided, plus delete and updating substates.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New states must be added to both allocateStateMachine() (Configure calls) and the StatusDetails resolver; the sync.Pool means the state machine is reused — always reset Invoice before use.

key_file_guides.role: InvoicePendingLines and prepareBillableLines — the core invoice-creation flow including progressive billing line splitting, collection cutoff resolution, and per-currency invoice creation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: resolvePendingLineCollectionCutoff has three alignment modes (bypass, subscription, anchored) — new alignment kinds must be added here.

key_file_guides.role: SnapshotLineQuantities, snapshotLineQuantitiesInParallel (semaphore-bounded), getFeatureUsage (pre-line and line-period meter queries for split lines).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Avg/Min meter aggregations are not supported for split lines — validate in getFeatureUsage.Validate(); semaphore size is maxParallelQuantitySnapshots from Config.

key_file_guides.role: Pure stateless calculation pipeline (see child summary); injected as invoicecalc.Calculator into Service; mockable via WithInvoiceCalculator.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All dependency data (FeatureMeters, TaxCodes, RatingService) must be resolved before calling Calculator.Calculate — the pipeline is stateless and cannot perform I/O.

key_file_guides.role: CreatePendingInvoiceLines: validates lines, resolves/upserts gathering invoice, normalizes and upserts lines, publishes GatheringInvoiceCreated event.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Lines with ChargeID set must have Engine explicitly set — validation enforces this; engine routing is done via lineEngines.populateGatheringLineEngine.

key_file_guides.role: Profile CRUD, ProvisionDefaultBillingProfile, demoteDefaultProfile, handleDefaultProfileChange (auto-pinning customers when invoicing app type changes).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Changing the default profile triggers customer re-pinning logic in handleDefaultProfileChange; all apps in a profile must have the same AppID (enforced by lo.Uniq check).

key_file_guides.role: resolveTaxCodes: collects unique Stripe tax codes from invoice lines and DefaultTaxConfig, looks them up (or creates them) via taxCodeService.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ReadOnly=true path silently skips missing codes (for simulate); ReadOnly=false creates codes (for real flows) — never use ReadOnly=false from simulate.

anti_patterns: Calling billing.Adapter methods directly without transaction.Run/RunWithNoValue — bypasses Ent ctx-bound transaction
Mutating customer invoices without calling transactionForInvoiceManipulation — omits advisory lock and leaves race conditions
Calling resolveTaxCodes with ReadOnly=false from simulate or preview code paths — creates tax code rows unexpectedly
Adding a new StandardInvoiceStatus without configuring it in allocateStateMachine() — state machine panics on unknown state
Importing billinglineengine from outside billingservice for direct line computation — always go through billing.Service.RegisterLineEngine and the LineEngine interface

decisions.decision: sync.Pool for InvoiceStateMachine to reduce GC pressure on high-frequency invoice advancement
decisions.rationale: Invoice state machines are large structs with closure-bound state graphs; pooling avoids repeated allocation on the hot billing-worker path.

decisions.decision: transactionForInvoiceManipulation UpsertCustomerLock outside the transaction then LockCustomerForUpdate inside
decisions.rationale: The advisory lock row must exist before the SELECT FOR UPDATE can acquire it; the two-step pattern prevents deadlocks when concurrent invoice creation races.

decisions.decision: advancementStrategy allows queued vs inline state machine advancement
decisions.rationale: Production uses queued advancement via billing-worker Kafka events to avoid holding long transactions; tests use inline advancement for determinism.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new invoice mutation method that must use the customer lock
